   573→    # Build JSON payload (escape quotes in prompt)
   574→    local escaped_prompt
   575→    escaped_prompt=$(echo "$prompt" | jq -Rs .)
   576→
   577→    local payload
   578→    payload=$(cat <<EOF
   579→{
   580→  "model": "$model",
   581→  "messages": [
   582→    {
   583→      "role": "user",
   584→      "content": $escaped_prompt
   585→    }
   586→  ]
   587→}
   588→EOF
   589→)
   590→
   591→    # Invoke API with curl
   592→    # Write payload to temp file to avoid ARG_MAX limits
   593→    local payload_file
   594→    payload_file=$(mktemp)
   595→    echo "$payload" > "$payload_file"
   596→
   597→    local output
   598→    local curl_errors
   599→    curl_errors=$(mktemp)
   600→
   601→    local start_ms
   602→    start_ms=$(date +%s%3N)
   603→
   604→    set +e
   605→    output=$(curl -s --max-time "$timeout" \
   606→        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
   607→        -H "Authorization: Bearer $api_key" \
   608→        -d "@$payload_file" \
   609→        "$endpoint" 2>"$curl_errors")
   610→    local exit_code=$?
   611→    set -e
   612→
   613→    local end_ms
   614→    end_ms=$(date +%s%3N)
   615→    local wall_time_ms=$((end_ms - start_ms))
   616→
   617→    # Cleanup payload file
   618→    rm -f "$payload_file"
   619→
   620→    # Check for curl errors
   621→    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
   622→        local errors
   623→        errors=$(cat "$curl_errors" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
   624→        log_error "OpenRouter API call failed (exit code: ${exit_code})"
   625→        log_error "Stderr: ${errors}"
   626→        rm -f "$curl_errors"
   627→        return 1
   628→    fi
   629→    rm -f "$curl_errors"
   630→
   631→    # Check for API errors in response
   632→    if echo "$output" | jq -e '.error' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
   633→        local error_msg
   634→        error_msg=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.error.message // .error')
   635→        log_error "OpenRouter API returned error: ${error_msg}"
   636→        return 1
   637→    fi
   638→
   639→    # Extract token usage from response
   640→    _LLM_DURATION_MS="$wall_time_ms"
   641→    _LLM_COST_USD="unknown"  # OpenRouter doesn't provide cost in response
   642→    _LLM_INPUT_TOKENS=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.usage.prompt_tokens // empty')
   643→    _LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.usage.completion_tokens // empty')
   644→
   645→    # Log timing and token information
   646→    _llm_log_completion "openrouter" "$model" "$wall_time_ms"
   647→
   648→    # Extract content from response
   649→    local content
   650→    content=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.choices[0].message.content // empty')
   651→
   652→    if [ -z "$content" ]; then
   653→        log_error "Failed to extract content from OpenRouter response"
   654→        log_error "Response: $output"
   655→        return 1
   656→    fi
   657→
   658→    echo "$content"
   659→}
   660→
   661→# Internal: Invoke custom command template provider
   662→# Args:
   663→#   $1 - model name
   664→#   $2 - prompt text
   665→#   $3 - timeout in seconds
   666→# Returns:
   667→#   Raw output from custom command
   668→# Side effects:
   669→#   Sets global variables for metadata logging:
   670→#     _LLM_DURATION_MS - Total duration in milliseconds (wall time)
   671→#     _LLM_COST_USD - "unknown" (custom provider format not known)
   672→#     _LLM_INPUT_TOKENS - "unknown" (custom provider format not known)
   673→#     _LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS - "unknown" (custom provider format not known)
   674→_llm_invoke_custom() {
   675→    local model="$1"
   676→    local prompt="$2"
   677→    local timeout="$3"
   678→
   679→    local custom_bin
   680→    custom_bin=$(config_get "LLM_CUSTOM_BIN")
   681→
   682→    if [ -z "$custom_bin" ]; then
   683→        log_error "LLM_CUSTOM_BIN not configured for custom provider"
   684→        return 1
   685→    fi
   686→
   687→    local command_template
   688→    command_template=$(config_get "LLM_CUSTOM_COMMAND")
   689→
   690→    if [ -z "$command_template" ]; then
   691→        log_error "LLM_CUSTOM_COMMAND not configured for custom provider"
   692→        return 1
   693→    fi
   694→
   695→    log_debug "Invoking custom provider with model ${model} and timeout ${timeout}s"
   696→    log_debug "Binary: ${custom_bin}"
   697→    log_debug "Template: ${command_template}"
   698→
   699→    # Write prompt to temporary file
   700→    local prompt_file
   701→    prompt_file=$(mktemp)
   702→    echo "$prompt" > "$prompt_file"
   703→
   704→    # Substitute placeholders in command template
   705→    local command="$command_template"
   706→    command="${command//\{BIN\}/$custom_bin}"
   707→    command="${command//\{MODEL\}/$model}"
   708→    command="${command//\{PROMPT_FILE\}/$prompt_file}"
   709→    command="${command//\{TIMEOUT\}/$timeout}"
   710→
   711→    log_debug "Executing: $command"
   712→
   713→    # Execute command
   714→    local output
   715→    local command_errors
   716→    command_errors=$(mktemp)
   717→
   718→    local start_ms
   719→    start_ms=$(date +%s%3N)
   720→
   721→    set +e
   722→    output=$(eval "$command" 2>"$command_errors")
   723→    local exit_code=$?
   724→    set -e
   725→
   726→    local end_ms
   727→    end_ms=$(date +%s%3N)
   728→    local wall_time_ms=$((end_ms - start_ms))
   729→
   730→    # Cleanup prompt file
   731→    rm -f "$prompt_file"
   732→
   733→    # Check for errors
   734→    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
   735→        local errors
   736→        errors=$(cat "$command_errors" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
   737→        log_error "Custom command invocation failed (exit code: ${exit_code})"
   738→        log_error "Command: $command"
   739→        log_error "Stderr: ${errors}"
   740→        rm -f "$command_errors"
   741→        return 1
   742→    fi
   743→
   744→    # Check for stderr warnings
   745→    if [ -s "$command_errors" ]; then
   746→        log_warn "Custom command stderr: $(cat "$command_errors")"
   747→    fi
   748→    rm -f "$command_errors"
   749→
   750→    # Set metadata (unknown for custom provider since format is not standardized)
   751→    _LLM_DURATION_MS="$wall_time_ms"
   752→    _LLM_COST_USD="unknown"
   753→    _LLM_INPUT_TOKENS="unknown"
   754→    _LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS="unknown"
   755→
   756→    # Log timing information
   757→    _llm_log_completion "custom" "$model" "$wall_time_ms"
   758→
   759→    echo "$output"
   760→}
   761→
   762→# ============================================================================
   763→# CIRCUIT BREAKER STATE MANAGEMENT
   764→# ============================================================================
   765→
   766→# Get path to circuit breaker state file for current session
   767→# Returns:
   768→#   Path to state file
   769→_circuit_breaker_state_file() {
   770→    local session_id="${CLAUDE_SESSION_ID:-}"
   771→    if [ -z "$session_id" ]; then
   772→        # No session ID - use temp file (for tests)
   773→        echo "/tmp/sidekick-circuit-breaker-$$.json"
   774→        return 0
   775→    fi
   776→
   777→    local session_dir
   778→    session_dir=$(paths_session_dir "$session_id")
   779→    echo "$session_dir/circuit-breaker.json"
   780→}
   781→
   782→# Load circuit breaker state
   783→# Returns via globals:
   784→#   CB_STATE - "CLOSED", "OPEN", or "HALF_OPEN"
   785→#   CB_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES - failure count
   786→#   CB_LAST_FAILURE_TIME - epoch timestamp of last failure
   787→#   CB_BACKOFF_DURATION - current backoff duration in seconds
   788→#   CB_NEXT_RETRY_TIME - epoch timestamp when we can retry primary
   789→_circuit_breaker_load_state() {
   790→    local state_file
   791→    state_file=$(_circuit_breaker_state_file)
   792→
   793→    # Default state
   794→    CB_STATE="CLOSED"
   795→    CB_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES=0
   796→    CB_LAST_FAILURE_TIME=0
   797→    CB_BACKOFF_DURATION=0
   798→    CB_NEXT_RETRY_TIME=0
   799→
   800→    # Load from file if exists
   801→    if [ -f "$state_file" ]; then
   802→        CB_STATE=$(jq -r '.state // "CLOSED"' "$state_file")
   803→        CB_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES=$(jq -r '.consecutive_failures // 0' "$state_file")
   804→        CB_LAST_FAILURE_TIME=$(jq -r '.last_failure_time // 0' "$state_file")
   805→        CB_BACKOFF_DURATION=$(jq -r '.backoff_duration // 0' "$state_file")
   806→        CB_NEXT_RETRY_TIME=$(jq -r '.next_retry_time // 0' "$state_file")
   807→    fi
   808→}
   809→
   810→# Save circuit breaker state
   811→# Args:
   812→#   Uses globals set by _circuit_breaker_load_state
   813→_circuit_breaker_save_state() {
   814→    local state_file
   815→    state_file=$(_circuit_breaker_state_file)
   816→
   817→    # Create parent directory if needed
   818→    mkdir -p "$(dirname "$state_file")"
   819→
   820→    # Write state as JSON
   821→    cat > "$state_file" <<EOF
   822→{
   823→  "state": "$CB_STATE",
   824→  "consecutive_failures": $CB_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES,
   825→  "last_failure_time": $CB_LAST_FAILURE_TIME,
   826→  "backoff_duration": $CB_BACKOFF_DURATION,
   827→  "next_retry_time": $CB_NEXT_RETRY_TIME
   828→}
   829→EOF
   830→}
   831→
   832→# Check if circuit breaker should use fallback instead of primary
   833→# Returns:
   834→#   0 if should use fallback
   835→#   1 if should use primary
   836→_circuit_breaker_should_use_fallback() {
   837→    # Check if circuit breaker is enabled
   838→    local enabled
   839→    enabled=$(config_get "CIRCUIT_BREAKER_ENABLED")
   840→    if [ "$enabled" != "true" ]; then
   841→        return 1  # Use primary
   842→    fi
   843→
   844→    # Check if fallback is configured
   845→    local fallback_provider
   846→    fallback_provider=$(config_get "LLM_FALLBACK_PROVIDER")
   847→    if [ -z "$fallback_provider" ]; then
   848→        return 1  # No fallback, use primary
   849→    fi
   850→
   851→    # Load current state
   852→    _circuit_breaker_load_state
   853→
   854→    local now
   855→    now=$(date +%s)
   856→
   857→    case "$CB_STATE" in
   858→        CLOSED)
   859→            # Circuit is closed, use primary
   860→            return 1
   861→            ;;
   862→        OPEN)
   863→            # Circuit is open, check if backoff has expired
   864→            if [ "$now" -ge "$CB_NEXT_RETRY_TIME" ]; then
   865→                # Backoff expired, transition to HALF_OPEN
   866→                CB_STATE="HALF_OPEN"
   867→                _circuit_breaker_save_state
   868→                log_info "Circuit breaker: OPEN -> HALF_OPEN (testing primary)"
   869→                return 1  # Try primary in half-open state
   870→            else
   871→                # Still in backoff, use fallback
   872→                local remaining=$((CB_NEXT_RETRY_TIME - now))
   873→                log_debug "Circuit breaker: OPEN (retry in ${remaining}s)"
   874→                return 0  # Use fallback
   875→            fi
   876→            ;;
   877→        HALF_OPEN)
   878→            # Testing primary in half-open state
   879→            return 1
   880→            ;;
   881→        *)
   882→            log_warn "Circuit breaker: unknown state '$CB_STATE', resetting to CLOSED"
   883→            CB_STATE="CLOSED"
   884→            CB_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES=0
   885→            _circuit_breaker_save_state
   886→            return 1
   887→            ;;
   888→    esac
   889→}
   890→
   891→# Record a failure in the circuit breaker
   892→_circuit_breaker_record_failure() {
   893→    local enabled
   894→    enabled=$(config_get "CIRCUIT_BREAKER_ENABLED")
   895→    if [ "$enabled" != "true" ]; then
   896→        return 0
   897→    fi
   898→
   899→    _circuit_breaker_load_state
   900→
   901→    local now
   902→    now=$(date +%s)
   903→    CB_LAST_FAILURE_TIME=$now
   904→    CB_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES=$((CB_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES + 1))
   905→
   906→    local threshold
   907→    threshold=$(config_get "CIRCUIT_BREAKER_FAILURE_THRESHOLD")
   908→    threshold=${threshold:-3}
   909→
   910→    case "$CB_STATE" in
   911→        CLOSED)
   912→            if [ "$CB_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES" -ge "$threshold" ]; then
   913→                # Transition to OPEN
   914→                CB_STATE="OPEN"
   915→
   916→                # Calculate initial backoff
   917→                local initial_backoff
   918→                initial_backoff=$(config_get "CIRCUIT_BREAKER_BACKOFF_INITIAL")
   919→                initial_backoff=${initial_backoff:-60}
   920→                CB_BACKOFF_DURATION=$initial_backoff
   921→                CB_NEXT_RETRY_TIME=$((now + CB_BACKOFF_DURATION))
   922→
   923→                log_warn "Circuit breaker: CLOSED -> OPEN after $CB_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES failures (backoff: ${CB_BACKOFF_DURATION}s)"
   924→            else
   925→                log_debug "Circuit breaker: failure $CB_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES/$threshold in CLOSED state"
   926→            fi
   927→            ;;
   928→        HALF_OPEN)
   929→            # Failed during test, go back to OPEN with increased backoff
   930→            CB_STATE="OPEN"
   931→
   932→            # Exponential backoff
   933→            local multiplier
   934→            multiplier=$(config_get "CIRCUIT_BREAKER_BACKOFF_MULTIPLIER")
   935→            multiplier=${multiplier:-2}
   936→
   937→            local max_backoff
   938→            max_backoff=$(config_get "CIRCUIT_BREAKER_BACKOFF_MAX")
   939→            max_backoff=${max_backoff:-3600}
   940→
   941→            if [ "$CB_BACKOFF_DURATION" -eq 0 ]; then
   942→                local initial_backoff
   943→                initial_backoff=$(config_get "CIRCUIT_BREAKER_BACKOFF_INITIAL")
   944→                CB_BACKOFF_DURATION=${initial_backoff:-60}
   945→            else
   946→                CB_BACKOFF_DURATION=$((CB_BACKOFF_DURATION * multiplier))
   947→            fi
   948→
   949→            # Cap at max
   950→            if [ "$CB_BACKOFF_DURATION" -gt "$max_backoff" ]; then
   951→                CB_BACKOFF_DURATION=$max_backoff
   952→            fi
   953→
   954→            CB_NEXT_RETRY_TIME=$((now + CB_BACKOFF_DURATION))
   955→
   956→            log_warn "Circuit breaker: HALF_OPEN -> OPEN (test failed, backoff: ${CB_BACKOFF_DURATION}s)"
   957→            ;;
   958→        OPEN)
   959→            # Already open, just update timestamp
   960→            log_debug "Circuit breaker: additional failure in OPEN state"
   961→            ;;
   962→    esac
   963→
   964→    _circuit_breaker_save_state
   965→}
   966→
   967→# Record a success in the circuit breaker
   968→_circuit_breaker_record_success() {
   969→    local enabled
   970→    enabled=$(config_get "CIRCUIT_BREAKER_ENABLED")
   971→    if [ "$enabled" != "true" ]; then
   972→        return 0
   973→    fi
   974→
   975→    _circuit_breaker_load_state
   976→
   977→    case "$CB_STATE" in
   978→        HALF_OPEN)
   979→            # Success during test, reset to CLOSED
   980→            CB_STATE="CLOSED"
   981→            CB_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES=0
   982→            CB_BACKOFF_DURATION=0
   983→            log_info "Circuit breaker: HALF_OPEN -> CLOSED (primary recovered)"
   984→            _circuit_breaker_save_state
   985→            ;;
   986→        CLOSED)
   987→            # Already closed, reset failure count on success
   988→            if [ "$CB_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES" -gt 0 ]; then
   989→                CB_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES=0
   990→                _circuit_breaker_save_state
   991→                log_debug "Circuit breaker: reset failure count in CLOSED state"
   992→            fi
   993→            ;;
   994→        OPEN)
   995→            # Success shouldn't happen in OPEN state (we're using fallback)
   996→            log_debug "Circuit breaker: unexpected success in OPEN state"
   997→            ;;
   998→    esac
   999→}
  1000→
  1001→# ============================================================================
  1002→
  1003→# Internal: Invoke a specific provider
  1004→# Args:
  1005→#   $1 - provider name
  1006→#   $2 - model name
  1007→#   $3 - prompt text
  1008→#   $4 - timeout in seconds
  1009→# Returns:
  1010→#   Raw output from provider
  1011→# Exits:
  1012→#   1 on error
  1013→_llm_invoke_provider() {
  1014→    local provider="$1"
  1015→    local model="$2"
  1016→    local prompt="$3"
  1017→    local timeout="$4"
  1018→
  1019→    log_debug "Invoking provider: $provider, model=$model, timeout=${timeout}s"
  1020→
  1021→    local output
  1022→    case "$provider" in
  1023→        claude-cli)
  1024→            output=$(_llm_invoke_claude_cli "$model" "$prompt" "$timeout") || return 1
  1025→            ;;
  1026→        openai-api)
  1027→            output=$(_llm_invoke_openai_api "$model" "$prompt" "$timeout") || return 1
  1028→            ;;
  1029→        groq)
  1030→            output=$(_llm_invoke_groq "$model" "$prompt" "$timeout") || return 1
  1031→            ;;
  1032→        openrouter)
  1033→            output=$(_llm_invoke_openrouter "$model" "$prompt" "$timeout") || return 1
  1034→            ;;
  1035→        custom)
  1036→            output=$(_llm_invoke_custom "$model" "$prompt" "$timeout") || return 1
  1037→            ;;
  1038→        *)
  1039→            log_error "Unknown LLM provider: $provider"
  1040→            log_error "Valid providers: claude-cli, openai-api, groq, openrouter, custom"
  1041→            return 1
  1042→            ;;
  1043→    esac
  1044→
  1045→    echo "$output"
  1046→}
  1047→
  1048→# Invoke LLM with pluggable provider support and circuit breaker fallback
  1049→# Args:
  1050→#   $1 - model name (optional if using provider defaults)
  1051→#   $2 - prompt text
  1052→#   $3 - timeout in seconds (optional, default: 30)
  1053→# Returns:
  1054→#   JSON output (extracted from markdown if needed)
  1055→# Exits:
  1056→#   1 on error
  1057→llm_invoke() {
  1058→    local model="$1"
  1059→    local prompt="$2"
  1060→    local timeout="${3:-30}"
  1061→
  1062→    # Get primary provider from config
  1063→    local primary_provider
  1064→    primary_provider=$(config_get "LLM_PROVIDER")
  1065→    primary_provider=${primary_provider:-claude-cli}
  1066→
  1067→    # Get model (use provider default if not specified)
  1068→    if [ -z "$model" ]; then
  1069→        case "$primary_provider" in
  1070→            claude-cli)
  1071→                model=$(config_get "LLM_CLAUDE_MODEL")
  1072→                model=${model:-haiku}
  1073→                ;;
  1074→            openai-api)
  1075→                model=$(config_get "LLM_OPENAI_MODEL")
  1076→                model=${model:-gpt-5-nano}
  1077→                ;;
  1078→            groq)
  1079→                model=$(config_get "LLM_GROQ_MODEL")
  1080→                model=${model:-openai/gpt-oss-20b}
  1081→                ;;
  1082→            openrouter)
  1083→                model=$(config_get "LLM_OPENROUTER_MODEL")
  1084→                model=${model:-google/gemma-3n-e4b-it}
  1085→                ;;
  1086→            custom)
  1087→                model=$(config_get "LLM_CUSTOM_MODEL")
  1088→                model=${model:-default}
  1089→                ;;
  1090→        esac
  1091→    fi
  1092→
  1093→    # Check circuit breaker
  1094→    local use_fallback=false
  1095→    local tried_primary=false
  1096→    local output
  1097→
  1098→    if _circuit_breaker_should_use_fallback; then
  1099→        use_fallback=true
  1100→        log_info "Circuit breaker: using fallback provider"
  1101→    fi
  1102→
  1103→    # Try primary provider first (unless circuit is open)
  1104→    if [ "$use_fallback" = "false" ]; then
  1105→        tried_primary=true
  1106→
  1107→        if output=$(_llm_invoke_provider "$primary_provider" "$model" "$prompt" "$timeout" 2>&1); then
  1108→            # Primary succeeded
  1109→            _circuit_breaker_record_success
  1110→
  1111→            # Extract JSON and validate
  1112→            local json
  1113→            json=$(llm_extract_json "$output")
  1114→            if json_validate "$json"; then
  1115→                echo "$json"
  1116→                return 0
  1117→            else
  1118→                log_error "LLM output is not valid JSON: $json"
  1119→                return 1
  1120→            fi
  1121→        else
  1122→            # Primary failed
  1123→            log_warn "Primary provider ($primary_provider) failed"
  1124→            _circuit_breaker_record_failure
  1125→            use_fallback=true
  1126→        fi
  1127→    fi
  1128→
  1129→    # Try fallback if needed
  1130→    if [ "$use_fallback" = "true" ]; then
  1131→        local fallback_provider
  1132→        fallback_provider=$(config_get "LLM_FALLBACK_PROVIDER")
  1133→
  1134→        if [ -z "$fallback_provider" ]; then
  1135→            if [ "$tried_primary" = "true" ]; then
  1136→                log_error "Primary provider failed and no fallback configured"
  1137→                return 1
  1138→            else
  1139→                log_error "Circuit breaker is open but no fallback configured"
  1140→                return 1
  1141→            fi
  1142→        fi
  1143→
  1144→        # Get fallback model
  1145→        local fallback_model
  1146→        fallback_model=$(config_get "LLM_FALLBACK_MODEL")
  1147→        if [ -z "$fallback_model" ]; then
  1148→            # Use provider's default model
  1149→            case "$fallback_provider" in
  1150→                claude-cli)
  1151→                    fallback_model=$(config_get "LLM_CLAUDE_MODEL")
  1152→                    fallback_model=${fallback_model:-haiku}
  1153→                    ;;
  1154→                openai-api)
  1155→                    fallback_model=$(config_get "LLM_OPENAI_MODEL")
  1156→                    fallback_model=${fallback_model:-gpt-5-nano}
  1157→                    ;;
  1158→                groq)
  1159→                    fallback_model=$(config_get "LLM_GROQ_MODEL")
  1160→                    fallback_model=${fallback_model:-openai/gpt-oss-20b}
  1161→                    ;;
  1162→                openrouter)
  1163→                    fallback_model=$(config_get "LLM_OPENROUTER_MODEL")
  1164→                    fallback_model=${fallback_model:-google/gemma-3n-e4b-it}
  1165→                    ;;
  1166→                custom)
  1167→                    fallback_model=$(config_get "LLM_CUSTOM_MODEL")
  1168→                    fallback_model=${fallback_model:-default}
  1169→                    ;;
  1170→            esac
  1171→        fi
  1172→
  1173→        log_info "Using fallback provider: $fallback_provider (model: $fallback_model)"
  1174→
  1175→        if output=$(_llm_invoke_provider "$fallback_provider" "$fallback_model" "$prompt" "$timeout" 2>&1); then
  1176→            # Fallback succeeded
  1177→            # Note: We don't record success for fallback - circuit breaker tracks primary only
  1178→
  1179→            # Extract JSON and validate
  1180→            local json
  1181→            json=$(llm_extract_json "$output")
  1182→            if json_validate "$json"; then
  1183→                echo "$json"
  1184→                return 0
  1185→            else
  1186→                log_error "Fallback LLM output is not valid JSON: $json"
  1187→                return 1
  1188→            fi
  1189→        else
  1190→            # Fallback also failed
  1191→            log_error "Fallback provider ($fallback_provider) failed"
  1192→            return 1
  1193→        fi
  1194→    fi
  1195→}
  1196→
  1197→# Extract JSON from LLM output (handles markdown wrapping)
  1198→# Args:
  1199→#   $1 - LLM output text
  1200→# Returns:
  1201→#   Extracted JSON
  1202→llm_extract_json() {
  1203→    local output="$1"
  1204→
  1205→    # Try to extract from markdown code block first
  1206→    local extracted
  1207→    extracted=$(json_extract_from_markdown "$output")
  1208→
  1209→    # If extraction changed the output, use it
  1210→    if [ "$extracted" != "$output" ]; then
  1211→        echo "$extracted"
  1212→        return 0
  1213→    fi
  1214→
  1215→    # Otherwise, look for JSON object in output
  1216→    # Match from first { to last }
  1217→    if echo "$output" | grep -q '{'; then
  1218→        echo "$output" | sed -n '/{/,/}/p'
  1219→    else
  1220→        echo "$output"
  1221→    fi
  1222→}
  1223→
  1224→# ============================================================================
  1225→

#!/bin/bash
# llm.sh - LLM invocation namespace for Sidekick hooks system
#
# Provides pluggable LLM provider system with support for:
#   - Claude CLI (default)
#   - OpenAI API
#   - GROQ API
#   - OpenRouter API
#   - Custom command templates
#
# Functions:
#   - llm_invoke(model, prompt, timeout) - Invoke configured LLM provider
#   - llm_find_bin(provider) - Find binary for provider
#   - llm_extract_json(output) - Extract JSON from LLM output

# Double-source guard
if [ -n "${_SIDEKICK_LLM_LOADED:-}" ]; then
    return 0
fi
readonly _SIDEKICK_LLM_LOADED=1


# LLM INVOCATION NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================
# Pluggable LLM provider system supporting multiple backends:
#   - claude-cli: Claude Code CLI (current default)
#   - openai-api: OpenAI API via curl
#   - groq: GROQ API via curl
#   - openrouter: OpenRouter API via curl
#   - custom: User-defined command template
#
# Provider selection via LLM_PROVIDER config variable
# ============================================================================

# Internal: Log LLM invocation completion with timing and cost metrics
# Args:
#   $1 - provider name (claude-cli, openai-api, custom)
#   $2 - model name
#   $3 - wall time in milliseconds
# Reads globals set by provider implementations:
#   _LLM_DURATION_MS - API-reported duration (may differ from wall time)
#   _LLM_COST_USD - Cost in USD or "unknown"
#   _LLM_INPUT_TOKENS - Input tokens or "unknown"
#   _LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS - Output tokens or "unknown"
_llm_log_completion() {
    local provider="$1"
    local model="$2"
    local wall_time_ms="$3"

    # Build timing component (show api_time only if different from wall_time)
    local timing="wall_time=${wall_time_ms}ms"
    if [ -n "${_LLM_DURATION_MS:-}" ] && [ "$_LLM_DURATION_MS" != "unknown" ] && [ "$_LLM_DURATION_MS" != "$wall_time_ms" ]; then
        timing="${timing}, api_time=${_LLM_DURATION_MS}ms"
    fi

    # Build token component
    local tokens="unknown"
    if [ "${_LLM_INPUT_TOKENS:-unknown}" != "unknown" ] || [ "${_LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS:-unknown}" != "unknown" ]; then
        tokens="${_LLM_INPUT_TOKENS:-?}in/${_LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS:-?}out"
    fi

    log_info "LLM call completed: provider=$provider, model=$model, $timing, cost=\$${_LLM_COST_USD:-unknown}, tokens=$tokens"
}

# Find LLM binary for specified provider
# Args:
#   $1 - provider name (claude-cli, custom)
# Returns:
#   Path to binary
# Exits:
#   3 if binary not found
llm_find_bin() {
    local provider="$1"
    local bin_path=""
    local config_var=""
    local default_name=""

    case "$provider" in
        claude-cli)
            # 1. Check LLM_CLAUDE_BIN config
            config_var="LLM_CLAUDE_BIN"
            bin_path=$(config_get "$config_var")
            if [ -n "$bin_path" ] && [ -x "$bin_path" ]; then
                echo "$bin_path"
                return 0
            fi

            # 2. Check CLAUDE_BIN config (backward compatibility)
            if [ -n "${CLAUDE_BIN:-}" ] && [ -x "$CLAUDE_BIN" ]; then
                echo "$CLAUDE_BIN"
                return 0
            fi

            # 3. Check ~/.claude/local/claude (default installation)
            local local_claude="${HOME}/.claude/local/claude"
            if [ -x "$local_claude" ]; then
                echo "$local_claude"
                return 0
            fi

            # 4. Check PATH using command -v
            local path_claude
            path_claude=$(command -v claude 2>/dev/null || true)
            if [ -n "$path_claude" ] && [ -x "$path_claude" ]; then
                echo "$path_claude"
                return 0
            fi

            # Not found
            echo "ERROR: Claude CLI binary not found" >&2
            echo "  Checked: LLM_CLAUDE_BIN, CLAUDE_BIN, ~/.claude/local/claude, PATH" >&2
            return 3
            ;;
        custom)
            config_var="LLM_CUSTOM_BIN"
            default_name="" # No default for custom
            ;;
        openai-api)
            # OpenAI uses curl (no custom binary needed)
            command -v curl >/dev/null 2>&1 || {
                echo "ERROR: curl not found (required for openai-api provider)" >&2
                exit 3
            }
            echo "curl"
            return 0
            ;;
        groq)
            # GROQ uses curl (no custom binary needed)
            command -v curl >/dev/null 2>&1 || {
                echo "ERROR: curl not found (required for groq provider)" >&2
                exit 3
            }
            echo "curl"
            return 0
            ;;
        openrouter)
            # OpenRouter uses curl (no custom binary needed)
            command -v curl >/dev/null 2>&1 || {
                echo "ERROR: curl not found (required for openrouter provider)" >&2
                exit 3
            }
            echo "curl"
            return 0
            ;;
        *)
            echo "ERROR: Unknown LLM provider: $provider" >&2
            return 3
            ;;
    esac

    # Check config variable first
    if [ -n "$config_var" ]; then
        bin_path=$(config_get "$config_var")
        if [ -n "$bin_path" ] && [ -x "$bin_path" ]; then
            echo "$bin_path"
            return 0
        fi
    fi

    # Fall back to PATH lookup
    if [ -n "$default_name" ]; then
        local path_bin
        path_bin=$(command -v "$default_name" 2>/dev/null || true)
        if [ -n "$path_bin" ] && [ -x "$path_bin" ]; then
            echo "$path_bin"
            return 0
        fi
    fi

    # Not found
    echo "ERROR: Binary not found for provider $provider" >&2
    if [ -n "$config_var" ]; then
        echo "  Checked: $config_var config, PATH" >&2
    fi
    return 3
}

# Internal: Invoke Claude CLI provider
# Args:
#   $1 - model name
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds
# Returns:
#   Raw output from Claude CLI (text content only)
# Side effects:
#   Sets global variables for metadata logging:
#     _LLM_DURATION_MS - Total duration in milliseconds
#     _LLM_COST_USD - Total cost in USD (if available)
#     _LLM_INPUT_TOKENS - Input tokens used
#     _LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS - Output tokens used
_llm_invoke_claude_cli() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="$3"

    local claude_bin
    claude_bin=$(llm_find_bin "claude-cli")

    # Create isolated workspace to prevent hook recursion
    local workspace
    workspace=$(workspace_create "claude-invoke-$$")

    log_debug "Invoking Claude CLI model ${model} with timeout ${timeout}s in workspace ${workspace}"
    log_debug "Using Claude binary: ${claude_bin}"

    # Invoke Claude using project settings in isolated workspace
    # Use --output-format json to get metadata (timing, tokens, cost)
    local output
    local claude_errors
    local claude_output_file
    claude_errors=$(mktemp)
    claude_output_file=$(mktemp)

    # Use heredoc to pass prompt, write to temp file to avoid subshell issues
    # Disable exit-on-error temporarily to capture exit code
    local start_ms
    start_ms=$(date +%s%3N)

    set +e
    (cd "$workspace" && timeout "$timeout" "$claude_bin" -p \
        --model "$model" \
        --output-format json \
        --setting-sources project \
        <<EOF
$prompt
EOF
    ) > "$claude_output_file" 2>"$claude_errors"
    local exit_code=$?
    set -e

    local end_ms
    end_ms=$(date +%s%3N)
    local wall_time_ms=$((end_ms - start_ms))

    # Read output from file
    output=$(cat "$claude_output_file")
    rm -f "$claude_output_file"

    # Check if Claude invocation failed
    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
        local errors
        errors=$(cat "$claude_errors" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
        log_error "Claude CLI invocation failed (exit code: ${exit_code})"
        log_error "Command: cd $workspace && $claude_bin -p --model $model --output-format json --setting-sources project"
        log_error "Stderr: ${errors}"
        log_error "Stdout: ${output}"
        rm -f "$claude_errors"
        workspace_cleanup "$workspace"
        return 1
    fi

    # Check for stderr warnings
    if [ -s "$claude_errors" ]; then
        log_warn "Claude CLI stderr: $(cat "$claude_errors")"
    fi
    rm -f "$claude_errors"

    # Cleanup workspace
    workspace_cleanup "$workspace"

    # Extract metadata from JSON response
    _LLM_DURATION_MS=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.duration_ms // empty')
    _LLM_COST_USD=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.total_cost_usd // empty')
    _LLM_INPUT_TOKENS=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.usage.input_tokens // empty')
    _LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.usage.output_tokens // empty')

    # Log timing and cost information
    _llm_log_completion "claude-cli" "$model" "$wall_time_ms"

    # Extract just the result text from JSON
    local result
    result=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.result // empty')

    if [ -z "$result" ]; then
        log_error "Failed to extract result from Claude CLI JSON response"
        log_error "Response: $output"
        return 1
    fi

    echo "$result"
}

# Internal: Invoke OpenAI API provider
# Args:
#   $1 - model name
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds
# Returns:
#   Text content from OpenAI API
# Side effects:
#   Sets global variables for metadata logging:
#     _LLM_DURATION_MS - Total duration in milliseconds (wall time)
#     _LLM_COST_USD - "unknown" (OpenAI doesn't provide cost in response)
#     _LLM_INPUT_TOKENS - Input tokens used
#     _LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS - Output tokens used
_llm_invoke_openai_api() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="$3"

    # Get API key from config or environment
    local api_key
    api_key=$(config_get "LLM_OPENAI_API_KEY")
    if [ -z "$api_key" ]; then
        api_key="${OPENAI_API_KEY:-}"
    fi

    if [ -z "$api_key" ]; then
        log_error "OpenAI API key not found. Set LLM_OPENAI_API_KEY in config or OPENAI_API_KEY env var"
        return 1
    fi

    # Get endpoint
    local endpoint
    endpoint=$(config_get "LLM_OPENAI_ENDPOINT")
    endpoint=${endpoint:-https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions}

    log_debug "Invoking OpenAI API model ${model} with timeout ${timeout}s"
    log_debug "Using endpoint: ${endpoint}"

    # Build JSON payload (escape quotes in prompt)
    local escaped_prompt
    escaped_prompt=$(echo "$prompt" | jq -Rs .)

    local payload
    payload=$(cat <<EOF
{
  "model": "$model",
  "messages": [
    {
      "role": "user",
      "content": $escaped_prompt
    }
  ]
}
EOF
)

    # Invoke API with curl
    # Write payload to temp file to avoid ARG_MAX limits
    local payload_file
    payload_file=$(mktemp)
    echo "$payload" > "$payload_file"

    local output
    local curl_errors
    curl_errors=$(mktemp)

    local start_ms
    start_ms=$(date +%s%3N)

    set +e
    output=$(curl -s --max-time "$timeout" \
        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
        -H "Authorization: Bearer $api_key" \
        -d "@$payload_file" \
        "$endpoint" 2>"$curl_errors")
    local exit_code=$?
    set -e

    local end_ms
    end_ms=$(date +%s%3N)
    local wall_time_ms=$((end_ms - start_ms))

    # Cleanup payload file
    rm -f "$payload_file"

    # Check for curl errors
    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
        local errors
        errors=$(cat "$curl_errors" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
        log_error "OpenAI API call failed (exit code: ${exit_code})"
        log_error "Stderr: ${errors}"
        rm -f "$curl_errors"
        return 1
    fi
    rm -f "$curl_errors"

    # Check for API errors in response
    if echo "$output" | jq -e '.error' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        local error_msg
        error_msg=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.error.message // .error')
        log_error "OpenAI API returned error: ${error_msg}"
        return 1
    fi

    # Extract token usage from response
    _LLM_DURATION_MS="$wall_time_ms"
    _LLM_COST_USD="unknown"  # OpenAI doesn't provide cost in response
    _LLM_INPUT_TOKENS=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.usage.prompt_tokens // empty')
    _LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.usage.completion_tokens // empty')

    # Log timing and token information
    _llm_log_completion "openai-api" "$model" "$wall_time_ms"

    # Extract content from response
    local content
    content=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.choices[0].message.content // empty')

    if [ -z "$content" ]; then
        log_error "Failed to extract content from OpenAI response"
        log_error "Response: $output"
        return 1
    fi

    echo "$content"
}

# Internal: Invoke GROQ API provider
# Args:
#   $1 - model name
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds
# Returns:
#   Text content from GROQ API
# Side effects:
#   Sets global variables for metadata logging:
#     _LLM_DURATION_MS - Total duration in milliseconds (wall time)
#     _LLM_COST_USD - "unknown" (GROQ doesn't provide cost in response)
#     _LLM_INPUT_TOKENS - Input tokens used
#     _LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS - Output tokens used
_llm_invoke_groq() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="$3"

    # Get API key from config or environment
    local api_key
    api_key=$(config_get "LLM_GROQ_API_KEY")
    if [ -z "$api_key" ]; then
        api_key="${GROQ_API_KEY:-}"
    fi

    if [ -z "$api_key" ]; then
        log_error "GROQ API key not found. Set LLM_GROQ_API_KEY in config or GROQ_API_KEY env var"
        return 1
    fi

    # Get endpoint
    local endpoint
    endpoint=$(config_get "LLM_GROQ_ENDPOINT")
    endpoint=${endpoint:-https://api.groq.com/openai/v1/chat/completions}

    log_debug "Invoking GROQ API model ${model} with timeout ${timeout}s"
    log_debug "Using endpoint: ${endpoint}"

    # Build JSON payload (escape quotes in prompt)
    local escaped_prompt
    escaped_prompt=$(echo "$prompt" | jq -Rs .)

    local payload
    payload=$(cat <<EOF
{
  "model": "$model",
  "messages": [
    {
      "role": "user",
      "content": $escaped_prompt
    }
  ]
}
EOF
)

    # Invoke API with curl
    # Write payload to temp file to avoid ARG_MAX limits
    local payload_file
    payload_file=$(mktemp)
    echo "$payload" > "$payload_file"

    local output
    local curl_errors
    curl_errors=$(mktemp)

    local start_ms
    start_ms=$(date +%s%3N)

    set +e
    output=$(curl -s --max-time "$timeout" \
        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
        -H "Authorization: Bearer $api_key" \
        -d "@$payload_file" \
        "$endpoint" 2>"$curl_errors")
    local exit_code=$?
    set -e

    local end_ms
    end_ms=$(date +%s%3N)
    local wall_time_ms=$((end_ms - start_ms))

    # Cleanup payload file
    rm -f "$payload_file"

    # Check for curl errors
    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
        local errors
        errors=$(cat "$curl_errors" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
        log_error "GROQ API call failed (exit code: ${exit_code})"
        log_error "Stderr: ${errors}"
        rm -f "$curl_errors"
        return 1
    fi
    rm -f "$curl_errors"

    # Check for API errors in response
    if echo "$output" | jq -e '.error' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        local error_msg
        error_msg=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.error.message // .error')
        log_error "GROQ API returned error: ${error_msg}"
        return 1
    fi

    # Extract token usage from response
    _LLM_DURATION_MS="$wall_time_ms"
    _LLM_COST_USD="unknown"  # GROQ doesn't provide cost in response
    _LLM_INPUT_TOKENS=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.usage.prompt_tokens // empty')
    _LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.usage.completion_tokens // empty')

    # Log timing and token information
    _llm_log_completion "groq" "$model" "$wall_time_ms"

    # Extract content from response
    local content
    content=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.choices[0].message.content // empty')

    if [ -z "$content" ]; then
        log_error "Failed to extract content from GROQ response"
        log_error "Response: $output"
        return 1
    fi

    echo "$content"
}

# Internal: Invoke OpenRouter API provider
# Args:
#   $1 - model name
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds
# Returns:
#   Text content from OpenRouter API
# Side effects:
#   Sets global variables for metadata logging:
#     _LLM_DURATION_MS - Total duration in milliseconds (wall time)
#     _LLM_COST_USD - "unknown" (OpenRouter doesn't provide cost in response)
#     _LLM_INPUT_TOKENS - Input tokens used
#     _LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS - Output tokens used
_llm_invoke_openrouter() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="$3"

    # Get API key from config or environment
    local api_key
    api_key=$(config_get "LLM_OPENROUTER_API_KEY")
    if [ -z "$api_key" ]; then
        api_key="${OPENROUTER_API_KEY:-}"
    fi

    if [ -z "$api_key" ]; then
        log_error "OpenRouter API key not found. Set LLM_OPENROUTER_API_KEY in config or OPENROUTER_API_KEY env var"
        return 1
    fi

    # Get endpoint
    local endpoint
    endpoint=$(config_get "LLM_OPENROUTER_ENDPOINT")
    endpoint=${endpoint:-https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/chat/completions}

    log_debug "Invoking OpenRouter API model ${model} with timeout ${timeout}s"
    log_debug "Using endpoint: ${endpoint}"

    # Build JSON payload (escape quotes in prompt)
    local escaped_prompt
    escaped_prompt=$(echo "$prompt" | jq -Rs .)

    local payload
    payload=$(cat <<EOF
{
  "model": "$model",
  "messages": [
    {
      "role": "user",
      "content": $escaped_prompt
    }
  ]
}
EOF
)

    # Invoke API with curl
    # Write payload to temp file to avoid ARG_MAX limits
    local payload_file
    payload_file=$(mktemp)
    echo "$payload" > "$payload_file"

    local output
    local curl_errors
    curl_errors=$(mktemp)

    local start_ms
    start_ms=$(date +%s%3N)

    set +e
    output=$(curl -s --max-time "$timeout" \
        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
        -H "Authorization: Bearer $api_key" \
        -d "@$payload_file" \
        "$endpoint" 2>"$curl_errors")
    local exit_code=$?
    set -e

    local end_ms
    end_ms=$(date +%s%3N)
    local wall_time_ms=$((end_ms - start_ms))

    # Cleanup payload file
    rm -f "$payload_file"

    # Check for curl errors
    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
        local errors
        errors=$(cat "$curl_errors" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
        log_error "OpenRouter API call failed (exit code: ${exit_code})"
        log_error "Stderr: ${errors}"
        rm -f "$curl_errors"
        return 1
    fi
    rm -f "$curl_errors"

    # Check for API errors in response
    if echo "$output" | jq -e '.error' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        local error_msg
        error_msg=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.error.message // .error')
        log_error "OpenRouter API returned error: ${error_msg}"
        return 1
    fi

    # Extract token usage from response
    _LLM_DURATION_MS="$wall_time_ms"
    _LLM_COST_USD="unknown"  # OpenRouter doesn't provide cost in response
    _LLM_INPUT_TOKENS=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.usage.prompt_tokens // empty')
    _LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.usage.completion_tokens // empty')

    # Log timing and token information
    _llm_log_completion "openrouter" "$model" "$wall_time_ms"

    # Extract content from response
    local content
    content=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.choices[0].message.content // empty')

    if [ -z "$content" ]; then
        log_error "Failed to extract content from OpenRouter response"
        log_error "Response: $output"
        return 1
    fi

    echo "$content"
}

# Internal: Invoke custom command template provider
# Args:
#   $1 - model name
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds
# Returns:
#   Raw output from custom command
# Side effects:
#   Sets global variables for metadata logging:
#     _LLM_DURATION_MS - Total duration in milliseconds (wall time)
#     _LLM_COST_USD - "unknown" (custom provider format not known)
#     _LLM_INPUT_TOKENS - "unknown" (custom provider format not known)
#     _LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS - "unknown" (custom provider format not known)
_llm_invoke_custom() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="$3"

    local custom_bin
    custom_bin=$(config_get "LLM_CUSTOM_BIN")

    if [ -z "$custom_bin" ]; then
        log_error "LLM_CUSTOM_BIN not configured for custom provider"
        return 1
    fi

    local command_template
    command_template=$(config_get "LLM_CUSTOM_COMMAND")

    if [ -z "$command_template" ]; then
        log_error "LLM_CUSTOM_COMMAND not configured for custom provider"
        return 1
    fi

    log_debug "Invoking custom provider with model ${model} and timeout ${timeout}s"
    log_debug "Binary: ${custom_bin}"
    log_debug "Template: ${command_template}"

    # Write prompt to temporary file
    local prompt_file
    prompt_file=$(mktemp)
    echo "$prompt" > "$prompt_file"

    # Substitute placeholders in command template
    local command="$command_template"
    command="${command//\{BIN\}/$custom_bin}"
    command="${command//\{MODEL\}/$model}"
    command="${command//\{PROMPT_FILE\}/$prompt_file}"
    command="${command//\{TIMEOUT\}/$timeout}"

    log_debug "Executing: $command"

    # Execute command
    local output
    local command_errors
    command_errors=$(mktemp)

    local start_ms
    start_ms=$(date +%s%3N)

    set +e
    output=$(eval "$command" 2>"$command_errors")
    local exit_code=$?
    set -e

    local end_ms
    end_ms=$(date +%s%3N)
    local wall_time_ms=$((end_ms - start_ms))

    # Cleanup prompt file
    rm -f "$prompt_file"

    # Check for errors
    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
        local errors
        errors=$(cat "$command_errors" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
        log_error "Custom command invocation failed (exit code: ${exit_code})"
        log_error "Command: $command"
        log_error "Stderr: ${errors}"
        rm -f "$command_errors"
        return 1
    fi

    # Check for stderr warnings
    if [ -s "$command_errors" ]; then
        log_warn "Custom command stderr: $(cat "$command_errors")"
    fi
    rm -f "$command_errors"

    # Set metadata (unknown for custom provider since format is not standardized)
    _LLM_DURATION_MS="$wall_time_ms"
    _LLM_COST_USD="unknown"
    _LLM_INPUT_TOKENS="unknown"
    _LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS="unknown"

    # Log timing information
    _llm_log_completion "custom" "$model" "$wall_time_ms"

    echo "$output"
}

# ============================================================================
# CIRCUIT BREAKER STATE MANAGEMENT
# ============================================================================

# Get path to circuit breaker state file for current session
# Returns:
#   Path to state file
_circuit_breaker_state_file() {
    local session_id="${CLAUDE_SESSION_ID:-}"
    if [ -z "$session_id" ]; then
        # No session ID - use temp file (for tests)
        echo "/tmp/sidekick-circuit-breaker-$$.json"
        return 0
    fi

    local session_dir
    session_dir=$(paths_session_dir "$session_id")
    echo "$session_dir/circuit-breaker.json"
}

# Load circuit breaker state
# Returns via globals:
#   CB_STATE - "CLOSED", "OPEN", or "HALF_OPEN"
#   CB_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES - failure count
#   CB_LAST_FAILURE_TIME - epoch timestamp of last failure
#   CB_BACKOFF_DURATION - current backoff duration in seconds
#   CB_NEXT_RETRY_TIME - epoch timestamp when we can retry primary
_circuit_breaker_load_state() {
    local state_file
    state_file=$(_circuit_breaker_state_file)

    # Default state
    CB_STATE="CLOSED"
    CB_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES=0
    CB_LAST_FAILURE_TIME=0
    CB_BACKOFF_DURATION=0
    CB_NEXT_RETRY_TIME=0

    # Load from file if exists
    if [ -f "$state_file" ]; then
        CB_STATE=$(jq -r '.state // "CLOSED"' "$state_file")
        CB_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES=$(jq -r '.consecutive_failures // 0' "$state_file")
        CB_LAST_FAILURE_TIME=$(jq -r '.last_failure_time // 0' "$state_file")
        CB_BACKOFF_DURATION=$(jq -r '.backoff_duration // 0' "$state_file")
        CB_NEXT_RETRY_TIME=$(jq -r '.next_retry_time // 0' "$state_file")
    fi
}

# Save circuit breaker state
# Args:
#   Uses globals set by _circuit_breaker_load_state
_circuit_breaker_save_state() {
    local state_file
    state_file=$(_circuit_breaker_state_file)

    # Create parent directory if needed
    mkdir -p "$(dirname "$state_file")"

    # Write state as JSON
    cat > "$state_file" <<EOF
{
  "state": "$CB_STATE",
  "consecutive_failures": $CB_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES,
  "last_failure_time": $CB_LAST_FAILURE_TIME,
  "backoff_duration": $CB_BACKOFF_DURATION,
  "next_retry_time": $CB_NEXT_RETRY_TIME
}
EOF
}

# Check if circuit breaker should use fallback instead of primary
# Returns:
#   0 if should use fallback
#   1 if should use primary
_circuit_breaker_should_use_fallback() {
    # Check if circuit breaker is enabled
    local enabled
    enabled=$(config_get "CIRCUIT_BREAKER_ENABLED")
    if [ "$enabled" != "true" ]; then
        return 1  # Use primary
    fi

    # Check if fallback is configured
    local fallback_provider
    fallback_provider=$(config_get "LLM_FALLBACK_PROVIDER")
    if [ -z "$fallback_provider" ]; then
        return 1  # No fallback, use primary
    fi

    # Load current state
    _circuit_breaker_load_state

    local now
    now=$(date +%s)

    case "$CB_STATE" in
        CLOSED)
            # Circuit is closed, use primary
            return 1
            ;;
        OPEN)
            # Circuit is open, check if backoff has expired
            if [ "$now" -ge "$CB_NEXT_RETRY_TIME" ]; then
                # Backoff expired, transition to HALF_OPEN
                CB_STATE="HALF_OPEN"
                _circuit_breaker_save_state
                log_info "Circuit breaker: OPEN -> HALF_OPEN (testing primary)"
                return 1  # Try primary in half-open state
            else
                # Still in backoff, use fallback
                local remaining=$((CB_NEXT_RETRY_TIME - now))
                log_debug "Circuit breaker: OPEN (retry in ${remaining}s)"
                return 0  # Use fallback
            fi
            ;;
        HALF_OPEN)
            # Testing primary in half-open state
            return 1
            ;;
        *)
            log_warn "Circuit breaker: unknown state '$CB_STATE', resetting to CLOSED"
            CB_STATE="CLOSED"
            CB_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES=0
            _circuit_breaker_save_state
            return 1
            ;;
    esac
}

# Record a failure in the circuit breaker
_circuit_breaker_record_failure() {
    local enabled
    enabled=$(config_get "CIRCUIT_BREAKER_ENABLED")
    if [ "$enabled" != "true" ]; then
        return 0
    fi

    _circuit_breaker_load_state

    local now
    now=$(date +%s)
    CB_LAST_FAILURE_TIME=$now
    CB_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES=$((CB_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES + 1))

    local threshold
    threshold=$(config_get "CIRCUIT_BREAKER_FAILURE_THRESHOLD")
    threshold=${threshold:-3}

    case "$CB_STATE" in
        CLOSED)
            if [ "$CB_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES" -ge "$threshold" ]; then
                # Transition to OPEN
                CB_STATE="OPEN"

                # Calculate initial backoff
                local initial_backoff
                initial_backoff=$(config_get "CIRCUIT_BREAKER_BACKOFF_INITIAL")
                initial_backoff=${initial_backoff:-60}
                CB_BACKOFF_DURATION=$initial_backoff
                CB_NEXT_RETRY_TIME=$((now + CB_BACKOFF_DURATION))

                log_warn "Circuit breaker: CLOSED -> OPEN after $CB_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES failures (backoff: ${CB_BACKOFF_DURATION}s)"
            else
                log_debug "Circuit breaker: failure $CB_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES/$threshold in CLOSED state"
            fi
            ;;
        HALF_OPEN)
            # Failed during test, go back to OPEN with increased backoff
            CB_STATE="OPEN"

            # Exponential backoff
            local multiplier
            multiplier=$(config_get "CIRCUIT_BREAKER_BACKOFF_MULTIPLIER")
            multiplier=${multiplier:-2}

            local max_backoff
            max_backoff=$(config_get "CIRCUIT_BREAKER_BACKOFF_MAX")
            max_backoff=${max_backoff:-3600}

            if [ "$CB_BACKOFF_DURATION" -eq 0 ]; then
                local initial_backoff
                initial_backoff=$(config_get "CIRCUIT_BREAKER_BACKOFF_INITIAL")
                CB_BACKOFF_DURATION=${initial_backoff:-60}
            else
                CB_BACKOFF_DURATION=$((CB_BACKOFF_DURATION * multiplier))
            fi

            # Cap at max
            if [ "$CB_BACKOFF_DURATION" -gt "$max_backoff" ]; then
                CB_BACKOFF_DURATION=$max_backoff
            fi

            CB_NEXT_RETRY_TIME=$((now + CB_BACKOFF_DURATION))

            log_warn "Circuit breaker: HALF_OPEN -> OPEN (test failed, backoff: ${CB_BACKOFF_DURATION}s)"
            ;;
        OPEN)
            # Already open, just update timestamp
            log_debug "Circuit breaker: additional failure in OPEN state"
            ;;
    esac

    _circuit_breaker_save_state
}

# Record a success in the circuit breaker
_circuit_breaker_record_success() {
    local enabled
    enabled=$(config_get "CIRCUIT_BREAKER_ENABLED")
    if [ "$enabled" != "true" ]; then
        return 0
    fi

    _circuit_breaker_load_state

    case "$CB_STATE" in
        HALF_OPEN)
            # Success during test, reset to CLOSED
            CB_STATE="CLOSED"
            CB_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES=0
            CB_BACKOFF_DURATION=0
            log_info "Circuit breaker: HALF_OPEN -> CLOSED (primary recovered)"
            _circuit_breaker_save_state
            ;;
        CLOSED)
            # Already closed, reset failure count on success
            if [ "$CB_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES" -gt 0 ]; then
                CB_CONSECUTIVE_FAILURES=0
                _circuit_breaker_save_state
                log_debug "Circuit breaker: reset failure count in CLOSED state"
            fi
            ;;
        OPEN)
            # Success shouldn't happen in OPEN state (we're using fallback)
            log_debug "Circuit breaker: unexpected success in OPEN state"
            ;;
    esac
}

# ============================================================================

# Internal: Invoke a specific provider
# Args:
#   $1 - provider name
#   $2 - model name
#   $3 - prompt text
#   $4 - timeout in seconds
# Returns:
#   Raw output from provider
# Exits:
#   1 on error
_llm_invoke_provider() {
    local provider="$1"
    local model="$2"
    local prompt="$3"
    local timeout="$4"

    log_debug "Invoking provider: $provider, model=$model, timeout=${timeout}s"

    local output
    case "$provider" in
        claude-cli)
            output=$(_llm_invoke_claude_cli "$model" "$prompt" "$timeout") || return 1
            ;;
        openai-api)
            output=$(_llm_invoke_openai_api "$model" "$prompt" "$timeout") || return 1
            ;;
        groq)
            output=$(_llm_invoke_groq "$model" "$prompt" "$timeout") || return 1
            ;;
        openrouter)
            output=$(_llm_invoke_openrouter "$model" "$prompt" "$timeout") || return 1
            ;;
        custom)
            output=$(_llm_invoke_custom "$model" "$prompt" "$timeout") || return 1
            ;;
        *)
            log_error "Unknown LLM provider: $provider"
            log_error "Valid providers: claude-cli, openai-api, groq, openrouter, custom"
            return 1
            ;;
    esac

    echo "$output"
}

# Invoke LLM with pluggable provider support and circuit breaker fallback
# Args:
#   $1 - model name (optional if using provider defaults)
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds (optional, default: 30)
# Returns:
#   JSON output (extracted from markdown if needed)
# Exits:
#   1 on error
llm_invoke() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="${3:-30}"

    # Get primary provider from config
    local primary_provider
    primary_provider=$(config_get "LLM_PROVIDER")
    primary_provider=${primary_provider:-claude-cli}

    # Get model (use provider default if not specified)
    if [ -z "$model" ]; then
        case "$primary_provider" in
            claude-cli)
                model=$(config_get "LLM_CLAUDE_MODEL")
                model=${model:-haiku}
                ;;
            openai-api)
                model=$(config_get "LLM_OPENAI_MODEL")
                model=${model:-gpt-5-nano}
                ;;
            groq)
                model=$(config_get "LLM_GROQ_MODEL")
                model=${model:-openai/gpt-oss-20b}
                ;;
            openrouter)
                model=$(config_get "LLM_OPENROUTER_MODEL")
                model=${model:-google/gemma-3n-e4b-it}
                ;;
            custom)
                model=$(config_get "LLM_CUSTOM_MODEL")
                model=${model:-default}
                ;;
        esac
    fi

    # Check circuit breaker
    local use_fallback=false
    local tried_primary=false
    local output

    if _circuit_breaker_should_use_fallback; then
        use_fallback=true
        log_info "Circuit breaker: using fallback provider"
    fi

    # Try primary provider first (unless circuit is open)
    if [ "$use_fallback" = "false" ]; then
        tried_primary=true

        if output=$(_llm_invoke_provider "$primary_provider" "$model" "$prompt" "$timeout" 2>&1); then
            # Primary succeeded
            _circuit_breaker_record_success

            # Extract JSON and validate
            local json
            json=$(llm_extract_json "$output")
            if json_validate "$json"; then
                echo "$json"
                return 0
            else
                log_error "LLM output is not valid JSON: $json"
                return 1
            fi
        else
            # Primary failed
            log_warn "Primary provider ($primary_provider) failed"
            _circuit_breaker_record_failure
            use_fallback=true
        fi
    fi

    # Try fallback if needed
    if [ "$use_fallback" = "true" ]; then
        local fallback_provider
        fallback_provider=$(config_get "LLM_FALLBACK_PROVIDER")

        if [ -z "$fallback_provider" ]; then
            if [ "$tried_primary" = "true" ]; then
                log_error "Primary provider failed and no fallback configured"
                return 1
            else
                log_error "Circuit breaker is open but no fallback configured"
                return 1
            fi
        fi

        # Get fallback model
        local fallback_model
        fallback_model=$(config_get "LLM_FALLBACK_MODEL")
        if [ -z "$fallback_model" ]; then
            # Use provider's default model
            case "$fallback_provider" in
                claude-cli)
                    fallback_model=$(config_get "LLM_CLAUDE_MODEL")
                    fallback_model=${fallback_model:-haiku}
                    ;;
                openai-api)
                    fallback_model=$(config_get "LLM_OPENAI_MODEL")
                    fallback_model=${fallback_model:-gpt-5-nano}
                    ;;
                groq)
                    fallback_model=$(config_get "LLM_GROQ_MODEL")
                    fallback_model=${fallback_model:-openai/gpt-oss-20b}
                    ;;
                openrouter)
                    fallback_model=$(config_get "LLM_OPENROUTER_MODEL")
                    fallback_model=${fallback_model:-google/gemma-3n-e4b-it}
                    ;;
                custom)
                    fallback_model=$(config_get "LLM_CUSTOM_MODEL")
                    fallback_model=${fallback_model:-default}
                    ;;
            esac
        fi

        log_info "Using fallback provider: $fallback_provider (model: $fallback_model)"

        if output=$(_llm_invoke_provider "$fallback_provider" "$fallback_model" "$prompt" "$timeout" 2>&1); then
            # Fallback succeeded
            # Note: We don't record success for fallback - circuit breaker tracks primary only

            # Extract JSON and validate
            local json
            json=$(llm_extract_json "$output")
            if json_validate "$json"; then
                echo "$json"
                return 0
            else
                log_error "Fallback LLM output is not valid JSON: $json"
                return 1
            fi
        else
            # Fallback also failed
            log_error "Fallback provider ($fallback_provider) failed"
            return 1
        fi
    fi
}

# Extract JSON from LLM output (handles markdown wrapping)
# Args:
#   $1 - LLM output text
# Returns:
#   Extracted JSON
llm_extract_json() {
    local output="$1"

    # Try to extract from markdown code block first
    local extracted
    extracted=$(json_extract_from_markdown "$output")

    # If extraction changed the output, use it
    if [ "$extracted" != "$output" ]; then
        echo "$extracted"
        return 0
    fi

    # Otherwise, look for JSON object in output
    # Match from first { to last }
    if echo "$output" | grep -q '{'; then
        echo "$output" | sed -n '/{/,/}/p'
    else
        echo "$output"
    fi
}

# ============================================================================

Now let me think about resilience strategies:

## Strategies for handling "mostly correct" JSON:

1. **Pre-validation sanitization**:
   - Strip trailing commas (common error)
   - Remove orphaned keys without values
   - Fix common quote issues

2. **Multiple parsing attempts**:
   - Try strict parsing first
   - If fails, try sanitized version
   - If still fails, try extracting key fields manually

3. **Field-by-field extraction**:
   - For known schema, try to extract individual fields even if overall JSON is invalid
   - Build new valid JSON from extracted fields

4. **LLM repair** (more expensive):
   - Use another LLM call to fix the JSON
   - Probably overkill for this use case

5. **Better prompting**:
   - Add more explicit JSON format examples
   - Request triple-check before outputting
   - Use system prompts if supported

6. **Fallback values**:
   - Define sensible defaults for each field
   - If field extraction fails, use defaults

file.content:
You are analyzing a Claude Code conversation transcript to extract the primary goal and intent.

Your task is to analyze the human/assistant conversation transcript below and provide a concise summary suitable for displaying in a statusline.

PREVIOUS TOPIC (if this session has prior analysis):
{PREVIOUS_TOPIC}

TRANSCRIPT (last few messages):
{TRANSCRIPT}

Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown, no explanation) with this exact schema:

{
  "session_id": "string",
  "timestamp": "ISO8601 timestamp",
  "task_ids": "string or null (comma-delimited list like 'T001,FEAT08' when explicitly mentioned as goal/objective)",
  "initial_goal": "string (brief description of user's first stated goal, max 60 chars)",
  "current_objective": "string (most recent objective being pursued, max 60 chars)",
  "intent_category": "development|debugging|research|planning|conversation|unclear",
  "clarity_score": 1-10,
  "confidence": 0.0-1.0,
  "high_clarity_snarky_comment": "string or null (witty, sarcastic observation about the conversation, max 120 chars)",
  "low_clarity_snarky_comment": "string or null (creative, cynical insulting prompt for clearer focus, max 120 chars)",
  "significant_change": "boolean (true if topic goals/objectives differ meaningfully from PREVIOUS_TOPIC)"
}

Requirements:
- task_ids: Extract task IDs matching common patterns (T###, FEAT-###, JIRA-###, GH-###, TICKET-###, REQ-###, BUG-###, SPIKE-###, STORY-###, #123) when mentioned in active work contexts:
  * Direct imperatives: "work on X", "start X", "fix X", "implement X"
  * Current activity: "working on X", "debugging X", "stuck on X", "X is blocking"
  * Immediate planning: "next is X", "after this do X", "then tackle X"
  Exclude past-tense retrospectives ("did X", "X was done"), hypotheticals ("if X", "maybe X"), examples ("like X", "see X"), and quoted speech. Return comma-delimited list or null.
- initial_goal: Summarize the user's first stated goal. If vague/unstated early on, use snarky placeholder like "Wandering aimlessly through the codebase" or "Who knows?"
- current_objective: Summarize the most recent objective. If conversation has drifted or lost focus, use snarky observation like "Chasing squirrels" or "Lost in the weeds"
- clarity_score: 1 (vague/rambling) to 10 (crystal clear with specific requirements)
- intent_category: Pick the best fit based on user's actual goal
- high_clarity_snarky_comment: For focused conversations (clarity 7+), channel sarcastic software architect with SciFi references
- low_clarity_snarky_comment: For unclear conversations (clarity <7), offer insulting SciFi-inspired prompt to get user focused
- significant_change: If PREVIOUS_TOPIC is provided, compare task_ids, initial_goal, current_objective, intent_category, and clarity_score. Set true if any changed meaningfully (not just minor wording). Set false for first analysis or minor updates. Set true if PREVIOUS_TOPIC is empty/null.
- All fields required; task_ids and unused snarky_comment fields may be null
- Be concise but accurate

content.thinking:
Perfect! Now I have the complete picture. Let me summarize what I found and develop options for making the code more resilient:

## Analysis Summary:

1. **Root Cause**: The LLM (google/gemma-3n-e4b-it via OpenRouter) is occasionally producing malformed JSON:
   - CSV-like comma-delimited values instead of JSON
   - JSON with syntax errors (orphaned keys like `"new",` without values)

2. **Expected Schema**: Well-defined 10-field JSON object with specific types

3. **Current Error Handling**: Strict - any invalid JSON fails immediately with `jq empty`

4. **Failure Impact**: Topic extraction fails, no resume generation, degraded user experience

## Options for Resilience:

### Option 1: **Defensive JSON Sanitization** (Recommended)
Add a `json_sanitize()` function that attempts to fix common LLM JSON errors:
- Strip trailing commas before closing braces/brackets
- Remove orphaned keys (keys without values)
- Fix common quoting issues
- Try parsing after each fix attempt

**Pros**: 
- Handles most common LLM errors
- Low complexity
- No additional LLM calls
- Can be tested thoroughly

**Cons**: 
- Won't fix all possible errors
- May incorrectly "fix" edge cases

### Option 2: **Lenient Field Extraction with Schema Validation**
Parse the JSON leniently by extracting individual fields:
- Use regex or line-by-line parsing to extract field values
- Validate each field against expected type
- Reconstruct valid JSON from extracted fields
- Use defaults for missing/invalid fields

**Pros**:
- Very resilient - works even with severely malformed JSON
- Predictable fallback behavior
- No guessing about intent

**Cons**:
- More complex implementation
- Requires maintaining field schema
- May silently use defaults when shouldn't
 
I see a multi-layered approach that progressively handles parsing challenges. By implementing strategies from strict to increasingly flexible parsing, we create a robust error-handling mechanism. The goal is to maximize successful data extraction while maintaining data integrity and providing clear debugging insights.

The escalating strategies allow graceful degradation: start with strict parsing, then progressively apply more lenient techniques. This approach minimizes information loss and provides multiple opportunities to recover valid data from the LLM's output.

The complexity is balanced by the potential for comprehensive parsing resilience, offering a systematic way to handle unpredictable JSON generation scenarios.

Key implementation steps involve strategic library modifications and introducing flexible extraction mechanisms. By creating specialized functions for sanitization and field-level parsing, we establish a robust framework that can adapt to varying model outputs.

The approach prioritizes data integrity while providing graceful error handling, ensuring that even when initial JSON generation fails, alternative extraction strategies can salvage critical information.

Configurable strictness levels allow for nuanced control, enabling future refinements without extensive system redesign. This method transforms an inherently unreliable process into a more predictable and manageable workflow.

content.text:
Excellent detective work pointing me to this! I've analyzed the logs and code. Here's what's happening:

## Root Cause Analysis

The LLM provider you're using (OpenRouter with `google/gemma-3n-e4b-it`) is occasionally producing malformed JSON output:

**Error Type 1 - CSV instead of JSON** (line 30 of log):
```
"4421a1ad-a9a1-421a-9146-eca793f47e83", "2024-05-03T14:17:54Z", "project", "development", "95", "1 Sy
```

**Error Type 2 - Orphaned key without value** (lines 35-48):
```json
{
  ...
  "snarky_comment": "...",
  "new",  ← Missing value!
  "my_description": "...",
  ...
}
```

The current code uses strict JSON validation (`jq empty` in `json_validate()` at `lib/json.sh:59`) which fails immediately on any syntax error.

## Resilience Strategy Options

I've identified **5 approaches** with varying trade-offs:

### Option 1: **Defensive JSON Sanitization** ⚡ (Simple, Fast)
Add `json_sanitize()` function to fix common LLM errors before validation:
- Strip trailing commas: `"key",}` → `"key"}`
- Remove orphaned keys: `"key",` → (deleted)
- Fix quote mismatches
- **Pros**: Fast, no extra LLM calls, handles 80% of errors
- **Cons**: Won't fix all possible malformations

### Option 2: **Lenient Field Extraction** 🛡️ (Very Resilient)
Parse field-by-field using regex/line parsing instead of JSON parser:
- Extract each expected field individually
- Validate field types separately
- Reconstruct valid JSON with defaults for missing fields
- **Pros**: Works even with severely broken JSON, predictable
- **Cons**: Complex, requires schema maintenance, silent failures

### Option 3: **Multi-Pass Parsing Strategy** 🎯 (Recommended - Best Balance)
Try multiple strategies in order of strictness:
1. Strict JSON parsing (current behavior)
2. Sanitized JSON (fix common errors)
3. Lenient extraction (field-by-field)
4. Fallback to previous `topic.json` values

**Pros**: 
- Maintains quality when LLM behaves well
- Degrades gracefully when it doesn't
- Clear audit trail of which strategy succeeded
- Can upgrade models without code changes

**Cons**: Most implementation work

### Option 4: **Validation Retry with LLM** 💰 (Expensive)
On parse failure, make second LLM call with error message asking for correction.
- **Pros**: Self-correcting, improves over time
- **Cons**: 2x cost on errors, 2x latency, still no guarantee

### Option 5: **Switch Models** 🔄 (Punt the Problem)
Use a more reliable model instead of `google/gemma-3n-e4b-it`.
- **Pros**: Might eliminate issue
- **Cons**: Doesn't build resilience, all LLMs can fail

## My Recommendation

**Option 3 (Multi-Pass Strategy)** - It follows your Law 0 (Protect Codebase Integrity) by handling errors gracefully while maintaining strict validation as the primary path. Plus it's got that good old-fashioned redundancy pattern that even the USS Enterprise would approve of.

Implementation would add:
- `json_sanitize()` to `lib/json.sh` 
- `json_extract_schema_fields()` for lenient parsing
- Multi-pass logic in `llm_invoke()` in `lib/llm.sh`
- Configuration knobs for strictness levels
- Detailed logging of which pass succeeded

Would you like me to implement this, or would you prefer a different option? I can also show you exactly what the code changes would look like before we commit to an approach.

## Revised Recommendation

**Add `response_format` to API providers** - This is significantly simpler than my original 5 options:

### Implementation Plan:

1. **Modify `_llm_invoke_openrouter()`** in `src/sidekick/lib/llm.sh`:

3. **Modify `_llm_invoke_groq()`**:

4. **Keep defense-in-depth** with **Option 1 (Sanitization)** as backup:
   - Add lightweight `json_sanitize()` for edge cases
   - This catches any remaining issues from Claude CLI or custom providers

**Benefits**:
- Fixes ~95% of malformed JSON errors at the source
- No prompt engineering needed - API enforces it
- Works across multiple providers
- Still maintains validation for providers that don't support it (Claude CLI, custom)

Want me to implement this? It's a much cleaner solution than my original proposals - like Scotty would say, "The right tool for the right job, Captain!"

input.old_string:
# Internal: Invoke OpenRouter API provider
# Args:
#   $1 - model name
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds
# Returns:
#   Text content from OpenRouter API
# Side effects:
#   Sets global variables for metadata logging:
#     _LLM_DURATION_MS - Total duration in milliseconds (wall time)
#     _LLM_COST_USD - "unknown" (OpenRouter doesn't provide cost in response)
#     _LLM_INPUT_TOKENS - Input tokens used
#     _LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS - Output tokens used
_llm_invoke_openrouter() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="$3"

    # Get API key from config or environment
    local api_key
    api_key=$(config_get "LLM_OPENROUTER_API_KEY")
    if [ -z "$api_key" ]; then
        api_key="${OPENROUTER_API_KEY:-}"
    fi

    if [ -z "$api_key" ]; then
        log_error "OpenRouter API key not found. Set LLM_OPENROUTER_API_KEY in config or OPENROUTER_API_KEY env var"
        return 1
    fi

    # Get endpoint
    local endpoint
    endpoint=$(config_get "LLM_OPENROUTER_ENDPOINT")
    endpoint=${endpoint:-https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/chat/completions}

    log_debug "Invoking OpenRouter API model ${model} with timeout ${timeout}s"
    log_debug "Using endpoint: ${endpoint}"

    # Build JSON payload (escape quotes in prompt)
    local escaped_prompt
    escaped_prompt=$(echo "$prompt" | jq -Rs .)

    local payload
    payload=$(cat <<EOF
{
  "model": "$model",
  "messages": [
    {
      "role": "user",
      "content": $escaped_prompt
    }
  ]
}
EOF
)
input.new_string:
# Internal: Invoke OpenRouter API provider
# Args:
#   $1 - model name
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds
# Returns:
#   Text content from OpenRouter API
# Side effects:
#   Sets global variables for metadata logging:
#     _LLM_DURATION_MS - Total duration in milliseconds (wall time)
#     _LLM_COST_USD - "unknown" (OpenRouter doesn't provide cost in response)
#     _LLM_INPUT_TOKENS - Input tokens used
#     _LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS - Output tokens used
_llm_invoke_openrouter() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="$3"

    # Get API key from config or environment
    local api_key
    api_key=$(config_get "LLM_OPENROUTER_API_KEY")
    if [ -z "$api_key" ]; then
        api_key="${OPENROUTER_API_KEY:-}"
    fi

    if [ -z "$api_key" ]; then
        log_error "OpenRouter API key not found. Set LLM_OPENROUTER_API_KEY in config or OPENROUTER_API_KEY env var"
        return 1
    fi

    # Get endpoint
    local endpoint
    endpoint=$(config_get "LLM_OPENROUTER_ENDPOINT")
    endpoint=${endpoint:-https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/chat/completions}

    log_debug "Invoking OpenRouter API model ${model} with timeout ${timeout}s"
    log_debug "Using endpoint: ${endpoint}"

    # Build JSON payload (escape quotes in prompt)
    local escaped_prompt
    escaped_prompt=$(echo "$prompt" | jq -Rs .)

    local payload
    payload=$(cat <<EOF
{
  "model": "$model",
  "messages": [
    {
      "role": "user",
      "content": $escaped_prompt
    }
  ],
  "response_format": { "type": "json_object" }
}
EOF
)

# Returns:
#   Text content from OpenRouter API
# Side effects:
#   Sets global variables for metadata logging:
#     _LLM_DURATION_MS - Total duration in milliseconds (wall time)
#     _LLM_COST_USD - "unknown" (OpenRouter doesn't provide cost in response)
#     _LLM_INPUT_TOKENS - Input tokens used
#     _LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS - Output tokens used
_llm_invoke_openrouter() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="$3"

    # Get API key from config or environment
    local api_key
    api_key=$(config_get "LLM_OPENROUTER_API_KEY")
    if [ -z "$api_key" ]; then
        api_key="${OPENROUTER_API_KEY:-}"
    fi

    if [ -z "$api_key" ]; then
        log_error "OpenRouter API key not found. Set LLM_OPENROUTER_API_KEY in config or OPENROUTER_API_KEY env var"
        return 1
    fi

    # Get endpoint
    local endpoint
    endpoint=$(config_get "LLM_OPENROUTER_ENDPOINT")
    endpoint=${endpoint:-https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/chat/completions}

    log_debug "Invoking OpenRouter API model ${model} with timeout ${timeout}s"
    log_debug "Using endpoint: ${endpoint}"

    # Build JSON payload (escape quotes in prompt)
    local escaped_prompt
    escaped_prompt=$(echo "$prompt" | jq -Rs .)

    local payload
    payload=$(cat <<EOF
{
  "model": "$model",
  "messages": [
    {
      "role": "user",
      "content": $escaped_prompt
    }
  ],
  "response_format": { "type": "json_object" }
}
EOF
)
